arts.ttl: Portugal 5-2 Denmark: Seleção make Nations League final four after thriller in Lisbon
arts.sum: They did it the hard way, but Portugal booked their place in the final four of the Nations League after a thrilling seesaw match against Denmark at the José Alvalade stadium tonight. Trailing by a single goal from the first leg, twice Portugal took the lead to level the tie on aggregate via a Joachim Andersen own goal and a Cristiano Ronaldo strike, but twice the Danes hit back to equalise on the night through Rasmus Kristensen and Christian Eriksen. With 90 minutes on the clock Portugal were heading out but substitute Francisco Trincão thumped in from the top of the box to send the tie into extra time. Trincão scored again right at the start of extra time with a terrific angled drive, and Gonçalo Ramos made absolutely sure it would be Portugal progressing with a late fifth goal. The Seleção will play Germany in the semi-final on 4 June in Munich. More to follow.

arts.ttl: Scotland stare down 8 game World Cup 2026 gauntlet as Denmark lie in wait for warp speed battle royale
arts.sum: World Cup qualifying is already underway for some teams as Steve Clarke looks to guide the national side back to a World Cup

arts.ttl: Punished for arguing! Italy concede bizarre goal, Ronaldo nets for Portugal
arts.sum: Italy conceded an embarrassing goal in their 3-3 Nations League quarter-final second-leg draw to Germany as the Azzurri were caught napping at a corner.

arts.ttl: Guinness World Records: the longest mustache, the loudest burp and Cristiano Ronaldo
arts.sum: There are Guinness World Records for almost anything these days, so Ronaldo being the holder of one puts him in some interesting company. Cristiano Ronaldo receives Guinness World Record

arts.ttl: Cristiano Ronaldo MISSES a shocking penalty for Portugal in Nations League clash with Denmark - hours after 40-year-old hit out at reporters for 'disrespectful' questions over his international future
arts.sum: Cristiano Ronaldo's struggles for Portugal continued on Sunday evening, as the 40-year-old missed a penalty just six minutes into his side's Nations League clash against Denmark.

arts.ttl: Cristiano Ronaldo responds to Rasmus Hojlund after Man United star mimicked his celebration
arts.sum: Hojlund scored the winner against Ronaldo’s Portugal side to put them in the driving seat in their Nations League quarter-final tie

arts.ttl: Cristiano Ronaldo gives toxic journalists an ultimatum: "Look me in the eye when I answer your questions"
arts.sum: The captain of the Portuguese national team, Cristiano Ronaldo, faced the media in a press conference, where he had no problem admitting that no one played well Portugal and Denmark meet again in the second leg, both teams want to fight for that place in the 'Final Four' of the Nations League.

arts.ttl: Cristiano Ronaldo loses his cool during tense press conference - 'Look me in the eyes'
arts.sum: Portugal are chasing a 1-0 deficit in their Nations League quarter-final second leg against Denmark on Sunday and Cristiano Ronaldo gave a frosty response to what he felt was "disrespectful" question at the pre-match press conference

arts.ttl: Portugal vs. Denmark: Cristiano Ronaldo sends out strong message ahead of UEFA Nations League showdown
arts.sum: Cristiano Ronaldo insists that he would be prepared to accept not being a starter for Portugal providing that the national team are winning.

arts.ttl: Fuming Cristiano Ronaldo hits back at reporters for asking 'disrespectful' questions as he demands one journalist to 'look me in the eyes'
arts.sum: Cristiano Ronaldo has clapped back at reporters for asking 'disrespectful' questions ahead of Portugal's crunch Nations League quarter-final second leg with Denmark.
